texto: Dinner was served on mismatched plates with paper napkins and silverware that looked like it had been stolen from a school cafeteria. The spaghetti was from a box that was still poking out of the garbage pail, the sauce from a jar that was sitting beside the sink. I got the definite impression that he chose to make dinner because he couldn't afford to take me out.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arlene Schindler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “Dinner was served on mismatched plates with paper napkins and silverware that looked like it had …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, dating, dinner pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dinner e take-out.
